cis: symbol. The function c i s (x) = cos ⁡ x + i sin ⁡ x {\displaystyle \mathrm {cis} (x)=\cos x+i\sin x}.
cist: noun. A small receptacle for sacred utensils carried in festivals in Ancient Greece.
din: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Dinka.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dimasa.
disc: noun. A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.
distinct: adjective. Capable of being perceived very clearly.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
ids: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idesa.
indict: verb. To accuse of wrongdoing; charge.
indicts: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of indict
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
sic: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Malinguat.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
stint: verb. To stop (an action); cease, desist.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tics: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tic
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titi: noun. Any New World monkey of the subfamily Callicebinae, native to South America, distinguished by their long soft fur.
